verbGlossary.ROLLS UP TO: {source}aggregate into summary activities or WBS nodes — the summary bar's dates and progress are derived entirely from its child detail activities
verbGlossary.DRIVES: {source} activity's completion is a prerequisite for {target} or event — the target cannot be achieved until the source finishes
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source} {target} time performance is quantified by the target — original duration, remaining duration, actual dates, and percent complete are tracked through the target's fields
verbGlossary.LINKED BY: {source} is connected to predecessors and successors through {target} (FS, SS, FF, SF) — the target defines how activities interact in the CPM network
verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: {source} follows {target} calendar's rules for working days, holidays, and hours — the target controls the duration-to-date conversion in all CPM calculations
verbGlossary.PRODUCES: {source} calculated output — the CPM forward and backward pass through activities produces early/late dates as computed results
verbGlossary.STRUCTURES: {source} naming and coding conventions provide the framework that organizes the target's dimensional classification — {target} IDs encode the trade, area, and phase that activity codes formalize into filterable dimensions
verbGlossary.MAPS TO: {source} corresponds to a level in {target} hierarchy — summary activities map to specific tiers (L1 executive, L2 phase, L3 area) in the multi-level schedule structure
verbGlossary.BASELINED BY: {source} milestone's planned date is frozen in {target} — the target preserves the approved date as the immutable reference for all future variance measurement
verbGlossary.SCORED BY: {source} element's performance is quantified by {target} — the target calculates a ratio or percentage that rates how well the source is performing against plan
verbGlossary.ENFORCED BY: {source} milestone's date is locked by {target} — the target overrides CPM logic to force the source to a specific date regardless of predecessor calculations
verbGlossary.OFFSET BY: {source} relationship includes a time delay or overlap defined by the target — the target's lag or lead value shifts the calculated dates beyond what pure logic would produce
verbGlossary.CATEGORIZED BY: {source} relationship is classified by the target into mandatory, discretionary, or external — the target's classification determines whether the relationship can change during delay analysis
verbGlossary.DETERMINES: {source} logic network defines the target — the sequence and type of predecessor/successor relationships determine which chain of activities forms {target}
verbGlossary.VALIDATED BY: {source} is checked for correctness by the target — the target identifies anomalies, missing connections, or discrepancies in the source
verbGlossary.INFORMS: Source classification provides essential context that the target analysis requires — the target's methodology depends on understanding whether {source}ic
verbGlossary.FLAGGED BY: Source anomaly is detected and reported by {target} check — the target's assessment identifies {source} deficiency requiring correction
verbGlossary.FEEDS: {source} metric contributes as an input to {target} — the target aggregates the source along with other indicators into an overall rating
verbGlossary.CONVERTS: {source} calendar translates the target's duration values into specific calendar dates — working day durations become date ranges through the source's workday/holiday definitions
verbGlossary.YIELDS: {source} calculation produces the target as a derived result — the difference between early and late dates from the CPM calculation yields total {target} and free float values
verbGlossary.REVEALS: {source} calculated dates expose the target — the forward and backward pass results identify which chain of activities has zero or least total {target}, revealing the critical path
verbGlossary.ADVANCES WITH: {source} data date moves forward in sync with the target update cycle — each monthly {target} advances the data date to the new status cutoff
verbGlossary.EXPOSES: {source} element reveals instances of {target} — the data date boundary identifies activities with progress that violates their logical predecessors
verbGlossary.STORED IN: {source} dates are preserved within the target as the approved reference — baseline dates are saved as an immutable snapshot inside {target} record
verbGlossary.DEFINES: {source} value establishes whether {target} exists — zero total float on an activity defines it as part of the critical path
verbGlossary.CONSUMED BY: {source} float is used up when {target} shifts activities within their available slack — resource leveling delays non-critical activities, reducing their remaining float
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} value is monitored across successive updates by {target} — the target measures the rate of change in the source over time to detect deterioration trends
verbGlossary.EXAMINED BY: {source} {target} path is studied in detail by the target analytical method — the target traces driving relationships, identifies convergence points, and evaluates near-critical alternatives
verbGlossary.INDEXED BY: {source} path length is expressed as a single-number ratio by the target index — the target divides {target} plus float by critical path to produce a completion probability indicator
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIED BY: Source element is converted into a measurable value by the target — {target} to {source}ing and trending
verbGlossary.COMPRESSED BY: {source} critical path duration is shortened through the target's recovery actions — {target}, or overlaps activities to reduce the source's total length
verbGlossary.CALCULATES: {source} earned {target} method produces the target performance index — the source's time-based measurement yields SPI(t) as its primary output metric
verbGlossary.REQUIRES: {source} analysis depends on the target data being present — earned {target} cannot be calculated without cost-loaded activities providing planned and earned value curves
verbGlossary.PROGRESSED BY: {source} baseline schedule is advanced through {target} — the target records actual dates and remaining durations against the source's planned values each period
verbGlossary.EVOLVES INTO: {source} schedule transforms into {target} lifecycle — the baseline schedule, through successive monthly updates, becomes the as-built record of actual construction
verbGlossary.COMPARED IN: {source} {target} is placed side-by-side with the as-built record in the target analysis — the target method examines planned vs. actual to attribute delay causes
verbGlossary.REFINES INTO: {source} monthly update is broken down into the target's finer-grained detail — {target} update feeds the three-to-six-week look-ahead with daily and weekly task specifics
verbGlossary.LOGGED BY: {source} update's modifications are recorded by {target} tracking system — every date shift, duration change, and logic edit in the source is timestamped in the target
verbGlossary.COORDINATED BY: {source} short-term plan is collaboratively developed through {target}y — the target's pull planning and constraint removal process shapes the source's weekly commitments
verbGlossary.ESCALATES TO: {source} condition triggers the target as a more aggressive response — when recovery scheduling cannot regain lost time, the situation escalates to acceleration with overtime and added crews
verbGlossary.ANALYZED BY: {source} as-built record is examined by the target retrospective method — the target applies as-planned vs. as-built, but-for, or windows analysis to the source's actual dates
verbGlossary.LEVELED BY: {source} resource demand is smoothed by {target}ing process — the target shifts activities within available float to eliminate peaks and valleys in the source's resource histogram
verbGlossary.JUSTIFIES: {source} delay classification provides the contractual basis for the target request — the source's categorization as excusable determines whether the target time extension has merit
verbGlossary.DENIED TRIGGERS: {source} time extension, when denied by the owner despite excusable delay, forces the contractor into the target acceleration — the hallmark of a constructive acceleration claim
verbGlossary.AGGREGATED INTO: {source} individual quality checks are combined into {target} — each of the source's pass/fail results contributes one point to the target's overall health rating
verbGlossary.COMPLEMENTS: {source} and target are complementary scheduling methodologies — takt time's fixed-rhythm zones and linear scheduling's location-based sequencing reinforce each other on repetitive work
verbGlossary.IMPORTS FROM: {source} visualization tool pulls {target} data from the target CPM authoring tool — the 4D/5D model links to activities imported from P6 or MS Project for sequence animation
verbGlossary.GENERATES: {source} methodology generates {target} as its primary output — the Last Planner System's weekly commitment tracking directly produces the Percent Plan Complete score
